abandon.en: leave a person or place permanently, especially when you should stay

ex.en: The family had to abandon their home during the flood.
ex.ko: 그 가족은 홍수 때문에 집을 버리고 떠나야 했다.

abandon.en: stop doing or continuing something you have started

ex.en: They abandoned the project due to a lack of funding.
ex.ko: 그들은 자금 부족으로 그 프로젝트를 포기했다.

abandon.cue: 규범을 신경 쓰지 않고 마음껏 하는
abandon.en: a lack of control or restraint, done freely without worrying about consequences

ex.en: She danced with wild abandon.
ex.ko: 그녀는 거리낌 없이 마음껏 춤을 췄다.

abroad.en: in or to a foreign country

ex.en: She studied abroad for two years.

abroad.en: located in or coming from a foreign country (usually placed after a noun)

ex.en: He has many friends abroad who visit every summer.
ex.ko: 그는 매년 여름 찾아오는 해외 친구가 많다.

absent.en: not present at a place where you are usually expected to be

ex.en: Three students were absent from school today.

absent.en: deliberately stay away from a place (formal, usually as 'absent oneself')

ex.en: He absented himself from the meeting without explanation.

abstract.en: existing as an idea rather than a physical or concrete thing

ex.en: Freedom is an abstract concept that is hard to define.

abstract.en: a short summary of an academic paper or article

ex.en: Please read the abstract before the full report.
ex.ko: 전체 보고서를 읽기 전에 초록을 먼저 읽어 주세요.

abstract.en: take out or summarize the essential points from something, such as a text

ex.en: The librarian abstracted the key findings from the report.
ex.ko: 그 사서는 보고서에서 핵심 내용을 요약했다.

academic.en: relating to education, schools, or universities

ex.en: She has a strong academic record.

academic.en: a person who teaches or does research at a college or university

ex.en: She is a respected academic in the field of physics.

active.en: doing a lot of things, or full of energy and action

ex.en: He stays active by going to the gym every day.
ex.ko: 그는 매일 헬스장에 가서 활동적으로 지낸다.

active.cue: 문법에서 주어가 동작을 하는 형태(수동태의 반대)
active.en: the grammatical form where the subject performs the action (opposite of passive)

ex.en: Write the sentence in the active, not the passive.
ex.ko: 그 문장을 수동태가 아니라 능동태로 써라.

adventure.en: an exciting or dangerous experience or activity

ex.en: They went on an adventure in the mountains.

adventure.en: risk something in the hope of a good result (literary)

ex.en: She adventured her savings on a new business.
ex.ko: 그녀는 저축한 돈을 새 사업에 걸었다.

affect.en: have an influence on someone or something, often causing change

ex.en: The heavy rain affected the football match.
ex.ko: 폭우가 그 축구 경기에 영향을 미쳤다.

affect.en: (psychology) the emotional response a person shows, as observed by others

ex.en: The patient showed a flat affect during the interview.
ex.ko: 그 환자는 면담 동안 무표정한 정동을 보였다.

age.en: the number of years someone has lived

ex.en: What is your age?
We live in the digital age.
He has aged a lot since I last saw him.
ex.ko: 그는 내가 마지막으로 본 이후로 많이 나이 들었다.

ex.en: She takes care of her aged parents.
The camp is for children aged 10 to 12.

aged.en: old people considered as a group

ex.en: The charity provides care for the aged.

ahead.en: in front, or forward in space

ex.en: I can see the mountains ahead.

ahead.en: before a particular time, or in the future

ex.en: We should plan ahead for the trip.
ex.ko: 우리는 그 여행을 미리 계획해야 한다.

ahead.en: in a leading position in a race or competition

ex.en: Our team is ahead by ten points.

ahead.en: in a leading position in a race, competition, or contest

ex.en: Our team is ahead going into the final round.
ex.ko: 우리 팀은 마지막 라운드를 앞두고 앞서 있다.

air.en: the mixture of gases that surrounds the Earth and that we breathe

ex.en: Open the window for some fresh air.

air.en: the space above the ground; the sky

ex.en: The bird flew high in the air.

air.en: broadcast a program on television or radio

ex.en: The new episode airs this Friday.

animal.en: a living creature that is not a plant, such as a dog, bird, or fish

ex.en: Lions are wild animals.

animal.en: relating to physical needs and instincts rather than the mind

ex.en: He acted on animal instinct rather than reason.
ex.ko: 그는 이성보다 동물적 본능에 따라 행동했다.

animate.en: give the appearance of movement or life to something, such as a drawing

ex.en: The studio animated the characters using computer software.
ex.ko: 그 스튜디오는 컴퓨터 소프트웨어로 캐릭터들에 생동감을 주었다.

ex.en: The story treats the wind as if it were animate.
ex.ko: 그 이야기는 바람을 마치 살아있는 것처럼 다룬다.

any.en: used to refer to one, some, or any amount of a thing, especially in questions or negatives

ex.en: Do you have any questions?

any.en: used before a comparative to mean 'at all' or 'to any degree'

ex.en: I don't feel any better today.
ex.ko: 나는 오늘 조금도 나아진 기분이 들지 않는다.

apart.en: separated by a distance, or into pieces

ex.en: The two houses are only a mile apart.
ex.ko: 그 두 집은 겨우 1마일 떨어져 있다.

ex.en: Apart from a few mistakes, the report was excellent.
ex.ko: 몇 가지 실수를 제외하면, 그 보고서는 훌륭했다.

apart.en: clearly different from others in quality

ex.en: Her talent puts her in a class apart.
ex.ko: 그녀의 재능은 그녀를 독보적인 위치에 올려놓는다.

appropriate.en: suitable or right for a particular situation

ex.en: Please wear appropriate clothing for the interview.

appropriate.cue: 허락 없이 남의 것을 가져다 쓰거나 자금을 따로 떼어 두다
appropriate.en: take something for your own use, often without permission, or set money aside for a specific purpose

ex.en: The government appropriated funds for the new hospital.
ex.ko: 정부는 새 병원을 위해 예산을 책정했다.

arctic.en: relating to the region around the North Pole

ex.en: Polar bears live in the Arctic region.

arctic.en: extremely cold, like the weather in the Arctic

ex.en: We faced arctic conditions during the winter hike.
ex.ko: 우리는 겨울 등산 중에 혹한의 상황을 마주했다.

arctic.en: the region around the North Pole

ex.en: Scientists are studying ice loss in the Arctic.
ex.ko: 과학자들은 북극의 빙하 손실을 연구하고 있다.

ex.en: The baby fell asleep in the car.

asleep.cue: 동작의 결과로 잠든 상태가 되도록(주로 -oneself asleep)
asleep.en: into a state of sleep, especially as a result of an action

ex.en: She cried herself asleep after the sad movie.
ex.ko: 그녀는 슬픈 영화를 보고 울다가 잠들었다.

assist.en: help someone by doing part of a task or providing support

ex.en: He assisted the nurse with the patients.

assist.en: (in sports) a pass or play that helps a teammate score

ex.en: He had two goals and one assist in the match.
ex.ko: 그는 그 경기에서 두 골과 어시스트 하나를 기록했다.

atlantic.en: relating to or bordering the Atlantic Ocean

ex.en: They took a cruise across the Atlantic Ocean.
ex.ko: 그들은 대서양을 가로지르는 크루즈 여행을 했다.

atlantic.en: the ocean between the Americas and Europe/Africa

ex.en: They sailed across the Atlantic in ten days.
ex.ko: 그들은 열흘 만에 대서양을 건너 항해했다.

auxiliary.en: providing extra help or support to something main

ex.en: The hospital hired auxiliary staff during the crisis.
ex.ko: 그 병원은 위기 동안 보조 인력을 고용했다.

auxiliary.en: a person who assists someone else in their work

ex.en: She works as a nursing auxiliary at the hospital.

average.en: the usual or typical amount, calculated by adding several numbers and dividing by how many there are

ex.en: The average temperature in July is 30 degrees.
He is an average student.

average.en: amount to or achieve a certain figure as an average

ex.en: The store averages about 200 customers a day.
ex.ko: 그 가게는 하루 평균 약 200명의 손님이 온다.

ex.en: The baby was awake all night.

awake.en: stop sleeping, or make someone stop sleeping

ex.en: She awoke to the sound of birds singing.
ex.ko: 그녀는 새들이 지저귀는 소리에 잠에서 깼다.

away.en: at a distance from a particular place, or moving from a place

ex.en: The beach is two miles away.

away.en: (of a sports game) played at the opponent's field, not your own

ex.en: The team won their away game against the Tigers.
ex.ko: 그 팀은 타이거즈와의 원정 경기에서 승리했다.

ex.en: The weather was awful all weekend.
That's an awful lot of money to spend.

bad.en: not good; unpleasant or of low quality

ex.en: The weather was really bad yesterday.

bad.en: something that is unpleasant, harmful, or of low quality

ex.en: You have to take the good with the bad in life.
ex.ko: 인생에서는 좋은 것과 나쁜 것을 다 받아들여야 한다.

bad.cue: 구어체로 badly 대신(want, need 등과 함께)

ex.en: He wanted to win that game bad.
ex.ko: 그는 그 경기에서 몹시 이기고 싶어 했다.

bag.en: a container made of cloth, plastic, or paper, used for carrying things

ex.en: She put her books in a bag.

bag.en: get or claim something, especially by being the first to do so (informal, British English)

ex.en: She bagged the last ticket to the concert.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 콘서트의 마지막 티켓을 차지했다.

ball.en: a round object used in games and sports

ex.en: The kids are playing with a ball.

ball.en: a large formal party where people dance

ex.en: They danced all night at the ball.

ball.en: form something into a round shape

ex.en: He balled up the paper and threw it away.

band.en: a group of musicians who play music together

ex.en: He plays guitar in a rock band.

band.en: a thin strip of material used to hold or decorate something

ex.en: She wore a hair band.

band.en: a group of people who share a common purpose

ex.en: A small band of rebels attacked the camp.

band.en: tie or fasten something with a band

ex.en: The workers banded the boxes together for shipping.
ex.ko: 작업자들은 배송을 위해 상자들을 띠로 묶었다.

ex.en: The door closed with a loud bang.

bang.en: hit something hard, making a loud noise

ex.en: He banged his fist on the table.

bang.en: hair that is cut short and hangs over the forehead

ex.en: She got bangs to change her look.
The arrow landed bang in the center of the target.

base.en: use something as the foundation or starting point for an idea or plan

ex.en: The movie is based on a true story.

base.en: the lowest or supporting part of something

ex.en: The base of the lamp is made of metal.

base.en: a place used as a center of operations, especially by the military

ex.en: The soldiers returned to their base.
He was driven by base motives like greed.
ex.ko: 그는 탐욕 같은 비열한 동기에 이끌렸다.

battle.en: a fight between armed forces during a war

ex.en: Many soldiers died in that battle.

battle.en: a long and difficult struggle to achieve or resist something

ex.en: She won her battle against cancer.

battle.en: fight or struggle against something difficult

ex.en: She has been battling cancer for two years.

bay.cue: 바다가 육지 쪽으로 굽어 들어온 곳
bay.en: a part of the sea or a lake that curves into the land

ex.en: They anchored the boat in the bay.

bay.en: (of a dog) make a long, deep howling sound

ex.en: The hounds bayed at the moon all night.
ex.ko: 그 사냥개들은 밤새 달을 보고 길게 짖었다.

beard.en: hair that grows on a man's chin and cheeks

ex.en: He grew a beard over the winter.

beard.cue: 위험을 무릅쓰고 상대의 근거지에서 정면으로 맞서다(관용구)
beard.en: boldly confront someone, especially in their own territory (from the idiom 'beard the lion in his den')

ex.en: He decided to beard his boss in her own office.
ex.ko: 그는 상사의 사무실로 직접 찾아가 대담하게 맞서기로 했다.

behind.en: at or to the back of something

ex.en: The cat is hiding behind the sofa.

behind.en: the part of your body that you sit on (informal)

ex.en: He fell right on his behind.

behind.en: having a lower score or lagging position in a race or contest

ex.en: They were behind by two goals at halftime.
ex.ko: 그들은 전반전에 두 골 뒤지고 있었다.

bellow.en: make a loud, deep shout or noise, especially in anger or pain

ex.en: He bellowed with anger when he saw the damage.
ex.ko: 그는 피해를 보고 화가 나서 고함쳤다.

bellow.en: a loud, deep shout or roar

ex.en: We heard an angry bellow from the next room.
ex.ko: 우리는 옆방에서 화난 고함 소리를 들었다.

belt.en: a strip of leather or cloth worn around the waist to hold up clothing or for decoration

ex.en: He wore a brown leather belt.
He belted the ball over the fence.
ex.ko: 그는 공을 담장 너머로 세게 쳐냈다.

bench.en: a long seat for several people, often found outdoors

ex.en: They sat on a bench in the park.

bench.en: (in sports) remove a player from the game or keep them from playing

ex.en: The coach benched him for the second half.

bent.en: curved or not straight, especially because of damage

ex.en: The bicycle wheel was bent after the crash.
ex.ko: 그 자전거 바퀴는 사고 후 휘어졌다.

ex.en: She was bent on winning the competition.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 대회에서 이기기로 굳게 결심했다.

bent.en: a natural talent or tendency for something

ex.en: She has a bent for languages.

best.en: of the highest quality or the most excellent (superlative of 'good')

ex.en: This is the best pizza I've ever had.
ex.ko: 이건 내가 먹어본 것 중 최고의 피자야.

best.en: the greatest effort or highest quality someone can achieve

ex.en: She always tries her best.

best.en: defeat or do better than someone

ex.en: She bested her opponent in the final round.

bet.en: risk money on the result of a game, race, or other event

ex.en: He bet fifty dollars on the horse race.

bet.en: be very confident that something is true or will happen

ex.en: I bet it will rain tomorrow.

bet.en: the money you risk on the result of an event, or the act of betting

ex.en: He placed a bet on the football match.
ex.ko: 그는 그 축구 경기에 내기를 걸었다.

better.en: of higher quality or more suitable (comparative of 'good')

ex.en: This new phone is better than my old one.
ex.ko: 이 새 휴대폰이 예전 것보다 낫다.

better.cue: 우열을 가릴 때 더 나은 쪽
better.en: the thing or side that is superior

ex.en: In the end, curiosity got the better of him.

better.en: do better than something done before, or improve something

ex.en: She bettered her personal record in the marathon.
ex.ko: 그녀는 마라톤에서 자신의 최고 기록을 경신했다.

big.en: large in size, amount, or degree

ex.en: They live in a big house.

big.en: in a big or important way (informal)

ex.en: The new movie is expected to hit big at the box office.
ex.ko: 그 새 영화는 흥행에서 크게 성공할 것으로 예상된다.

ex.en: The company is worth over a billion dollars.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 10억 달러 이상의 가치가 있다.

billion.en: an extremely large number (used to exaggerate)

ex.en: I've explained this a billion times already.
ex.ko: 나는 이걸 벌써 수도 없이 설명했다.

billion.en: used before a noun to mean one billion

ex.en: The project cost three billion dollars.

blanket.en: a large piece of thick cloth used to keep warm, especially in bed

ex.en: She wrapped the baby in a warm blanket.

blanket.en: cover something completely, like a blanket

ex.en: Snow blanketed the entire town overnight.
He has been blind since birth.

blind.cue: 무언가를 못 보거나 인정 안 하려는 (비유)
blind.en: unwilling or unable to notice or understand something

ex.en: She was blind to his faults.

blind.en: a covering for a window that can be raised or lowered to control light

ex.en: She lowered the blinds to block the sun.

blind.cue: 눈이 멀게 하다, 또는 판단력을 흐리게 하다(비유)
blind.en: make someone unable to see, or prevent someone from understanding clearly

ex.en: Love can blind you to a person's faults.
ex.ko: 사랑은 사람의 결점에 눈멀게 할 수 있다.

ex.en: The garden is full of blooming roses.

blooming.en: used to emphasize annoyance, especially in British English (informal)

ex.en: This blooming computer keeps crashing!

blooming.en: the process or period of flowers opening and blooming

ex.en: The blooming of the cherry trees attracts many tourists.

boiling.en: (of a liquid) very hot, bubbling as it heats

ex.en: Be careful, the water is boiling.
It was boiling hot on the beach yesterday.

boiling.en: the process of cooking food in boiling water

ex.en: Boiling is a quick way to cook vegetables.

bolt.en: a metal pin with a thread, used with a nut to fasten things together

ex.en: He tightened the bolt with a wrench.
The horse bolted when it heard the thunder.
ex.ko: 그 말은 천둥소리를 듣고 갑자기 뛰쳐 달아났다.

bolt.en: a metal bar used to lock a door, or the act of locking with one

ex.en: She slid the bolt to lock the door.
A bolt of lightning struck the old tree.

bolt.en: suddenly and completely straight or rigid

ex.en: He sat bolt upright when he heard the noise.
ex.ko: 그는 그 소리를 듣고 벌떡 꼿꼿이 일어나 앉았다.

bond.en: a close connection between people, based on shared feelings or experiences

ex.en: They share a strong bond as sisters.

bond.en: a certificate showing that money has been lent to a government or company, to be repaid with interest

ex.en: The government issued new bonds to raise money.
ex.ko: 정부는 자금을 마련하기 위해 새 채권을 발행했다.

bond.en: form a close relationship with someone

ex.en: The two dogs bonded quickly after meeting.
ex.ko: 그 두 강아지는 만난 후 빠르게 정이 들었다.

ex.en: There was a loud boom as the fireworks went off.
ex.ko: 불꽃놀이가 터지면서 쾅 하는 소리가 났다.

boom.en: a sudden increase in trade, business, or economic activity

ex.en: The country experienced an economic boom in the 1990s.

boom.en: grow or increase very quickly, especially in business

ex.en: The tech industry boomed in the early 2000s.

border.en: the line that separates two countries or areas

ex.en: They crossed the border into France.

border.en: the edge or outer part of something

ex.en: The photo had a white border around it.
ex.ko: 그 사진은 주위에 하얀 테두리가 있었다.

border.en: share a boundary with another country or area

ex.en: Canada borders the United States to the south.
ex.ko: 캐나다는 남쪽으로 미국과 국경을 접하고 있다.

both.cue: 둘 중 하나가 아니라 둘 모두
both.en: the two things or people mentioned, not just one

ex.en: Both of my parents are teachers.
ex.ko: 우리 부모님은 두 분 다 선생님이시다.

both.cue: 명사 앞에서 두 가지 모두를 가리켜
both.en: used before a plural noun to refer to two things or people together

ex.en: Both hands were covered in paint.

bound.en: tied tightly with rope, string, or similar material

ex.en: The prisoner's hands were bound behind his back.
ex.ko: 그 죄수의 손은 등 뒤로 묶여 있었다.

bound.cue: 어떤 일이 일어날 것이 거의 확실한

ex.en: If you don't study, you're bound to fail.
The flight is bound for Tokyo.
The rabbit crossed the field in a few bounds.
ex.ko: 토끼는 몇 번의 도약으로 들판을 가로질렀다.

ex.en: The dog bounded across the yard to greet us.
ex.ko: 그 개는 우리를 반기려고 마당을 껑충껑충 뛰어왔다.

brand.en: a particular product or company name that people recognize

ex.en: She only buys clothes from famous brands.

brand.en: describe someone in a critical way that makes people think badly of them

ex.en: The media branded him a traitor.

brave.en: having or showing courage in a difficult or dangerous situation

ex.en: The firefighter was brave to run into the burning building.
ex.ko: 그 소방관은 불타는 건물로 뛰어들 만큼 용감했다.

brave.en: face something dangerous or unpleasant with courage

ex.en: They braved the storm to rescue the sailors.

breakfast.en: the first meal of the day, eaten in the morning

ex.en: I usually have toast for breakfast.
We breakfasted early before the long hike.
ex.ko: 우리는 긴 등산 전에 일찍 아침 식사를 했다.

breeding.cue: 동물을 기르고 새끼를 낳게 하는 일
breeding.en: the process of keeping animals or plants to produce young ones

ex.en: The farm specializes in horse breeding.
ex.ko: 그 농장은 말 사육을 전문으로 한다.

breeding.en: used for or related to producing offspring

ex.en: The zoo has a special breeding program for pandas.
ex.ko: 그 동물원은 판다를 위한 특별 번식 프로그램을 운영한다.

bridge.en: a structure built over a river or road so people can cross it

ex.en: We crossed the bridge to get to the city.
ex.ko: 우리는 도시로 가기 위해 다리를 건넜다.

bridge.en: reduce the difference between two things or groups

ex.en: The new policy aims to bridge the gap between rich and poor.
ex.ko: 그 새 정책은 빈부 격차를 좁히는 것을 목표로 한다.

ex.en: It's a bright sunny day.
She is a bright student.

bright.en: in a bright way (informal, same as 'brightly')

ex.en: The stars shone bright in the night sky.

bronze.en: a reddish-brown metal made by mixing copper and tin

ex.en: The statue was cast in bronze.

bronze.en: a medal awarded for finishing in third place

ex.en: She won the bronze medal in swimming.

bronze.en: having the reddish-brown color of bronze

ex.en: She has beautiful bronze skin after her vacation.
ex.ko: 그녀는 휴가 후 아름다운 청동색 피부를 갖게 되었다.

brown.en: having the color of wood or coffee

ex.en: She has brown hair.

brown.en: the color of wood or coffee

ex.en: He painted the fence brown.

brown.en: cook food in a pan until it turns brown

ex.en: Brown the onions before adding the garlic.
ex.ko: 마늘을 넣기 전에 양파를 노릇하게 볶아라.

bubble.cue: 물이나 비눗물 속의 둥근 공기 방울
bubble.en: a small ball of air or gas inside a liquid or floating in the air

ex.en: Bubbles rose to the surface of the water.

bubble.en: a situation in which prices rise very quickly and then suddenly fall

ex.en: The housing bubble burst, and prices crashed.
The soup was bubbling on the stove.
ex.ko: 수프가 가스레인지 위에서 보글보글 끓고 있었다.

build.en: make something by putting materials and parts together

ex.en: They are building a new school.

build.en: the shape and size of a person's body

ex.en: He has an athletic build.

bull.en: an adult male of the cattle family

ex.en: The bull chased the farmer across the field.
ex.ko: 그 황소는 들판을 가로질러 농부를 쫓았다.

ex.en: He bulled his way through the crowd.
ex.ko: 그는 군중을 힘으로 밀어붙이며 뚫고 나아갔다.

bulletin.en: a short official statement or report, especially on the news

ex.en: A news bulletin interrupted the program.

bulletin.en: announce something publicly, as in a bulletin (rare)

ex.en: The results were bulletined on the school notice board.

bully.en: use strength or power to hurt or frighten someone weaker

ex.en: He was bullied at school for being different.

bully.en: a person who hurts or frightens someone weaker

ex.en: The bully picked on smaller kids.
ex.ko: 그 불량배는 더 작은 아이들을 괴롭혔다.

bully.cue: 옛날식 감탄 표현, 잘했다는 뜻('Bully for you!')
bully.en: very good (old-fashioned, mainly used in the phrase 'Bully for you!')

ex.en: "Bully for you!" he said sarcastically about her promotion.
ex.ko: 그는 그녀의 승진에 대해 비꼬듯이 "잘됐네!"라고 말했다.

bum.en: a person who is lazy or has no home or job (informal)

ex.en: He's been acting like a bum since he lost his job.
ex.ko: 그는 실직한 이후로 게으름뱅이처럼 지내고 있다.

bum.en: the part of your body you sit on (informal, especially British English)

ex.en: He fell right on his bum.
I got a bum deal on that used car.
ex.ko: 나는 그 중고차에서 형편없는 거래를 했다.

bum.en: ask someone to give you something for free (informal)

ex.en: Can I bum a cigarette off you?
ex.ko: 담배 한 대만 얻을 수 있을까?

bunch.cue: 포도·꽃 등이 여러 개 묶인 것
bunch.en: a number of things of the same kind that grow or are fastened together

ex.en: She bought a bunch of bananas.
A bunch of us are going to the beach this weekend.
ex.ko: 우리 중 몇 명이 이번 주말에 해변에 갈 거야.

bunch.en: gather or come together in a cluster

ex.en: The runners bunched up near the finish line.
Smoke rose from the burning building.

burning.en: extremely important and needing immediate attention

ex.en: There's one burning question everyone wants answered.
ex.ko: 모두가 답을 원하는 긴급한 질문이 하나 있다.

burning.en: the act or process of something being burned

ex.en: The burning of fossil fuels causes air pollution.
ex.ko: 화석 연료의 연소는 대기 오염을 일으킨다.

busy.en: having a lot of things to do

ex.en: I'm very busy this week.

busy.en: occupy yourself with a task (usually 'busy oneself')

ex.en: She busied herself with cleaning the house.
ex.ko: 그녀는 집 청소로 스스로를 바쁘게 만들었다.

cable.en: a thick wire or set of wires used to carry electricity or signals

ex.en: Connect the cable to the back of the TV.

cable.en: a television service delivered through cables

ex.en: We canceled our cable subscription.

cable.en: send someone a message by telegraph (old-fashioned)

ex.en: He cabled home to say he had arrived safely.
ex.ko: 그는 무사히 도착했다고 집에 전보를 쳤다.

ex.en: She stayed calm during the emergency.

calm.en: (of weather or water) without much wind or movement

ex.en: The sea was calm all morning.

calm.en: a state of being calm and free from stress

ex.en: She spoke with surprising calm during the crisis.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 위기 중에도 놀랍도록 침착하게 말했다.

calm.en: make someone or something calmer, or become calmer

ex.en: He tried to calm the crying baby.

can.en: a metal container for food or drinks

ex.en: She opened a can of soda.

can.en: used to say that someone is able to do something

ex.en: I can swim very well.
ex.ko: 나는 수영을 아주 잘할 수 있다.

can.en: preserve food by sealing it in a can

ex.en: They canned the tomatoes from their garden.
ex.ko: 그들은 텃밭에서 기른 토마토로 통조림을 만들었다.

cane.en: a stick used to help you walk

ex.en: The old man walked with a cane.

cane.en: the long stem of the sugar cane plant

ex.en: Sugar is made from cane.

cane.en: hit someone with a cane as punishment (especially in the past)

ex.en: In the past, teachers sometimes caned students for misbehaving.
ex.ko: 예전에는 교사들이 잘못한 학생을 회초리로 때리기도 했다.

cap.en: a soft hat, often with a curved part at the front

ex.en: He wore a baseball cap.

cap.en: a cover for the top of a bottle or container

ex.en: Put the cap back on the bottle.

cap.en: set a limit on the amount of something

ex.en: The government capped the price of gas.

career.en: the series of jobs a person has in a particular area of work over their life

ex.en: She had a long career as a teacher.
ex.ko: 그녀는 교사로서 오랜 경력을 가지고 있다.

career.en: move quickly and out of control (British English)

ex.en: The runaway truck careered down the hill.
ex.ko: 통제 불능이 된 트럭이 언덕 아래로 위태롭게 질주했다.

carry.en: move something from one place to another by holding or supporting it

ex.en: He carried the boxes upstairs.

carry.en: have something with you as you move around

ex.en: She always carries an umbrella in her bag.
ex.ko: 그녀는 가방에 항상 우산을 가지고 다닌다.

carry.en: (in golf) the distance a ball travels through the air before landing

ex.en: His shot had a carry of over 250 yards.

case.en: a particular situation or instance of something

ex.en: In that case, we should leave early.
ex.ko: 그런 경우라면 우리는 일찍 떠나야 한다.

case.en: a matter that is being dealt with by a court of law

ex.en: The lawyer won the case.

case.en: a container designed to hold or carry a particular thing

ex.en: She put her glasses in the case.

case.en: look closely at a place, usually planning to rob it (informal)

ex.en: The thieves cased the bank before the robbery.
ex.ko: 도둑들은 강도짓을 하기 전에 그 은행을 미리 살펴봤다.

caution.en: great care taken to avoid danger or mistakes

ex.en: Handle the chemicals with caution.

caution.en: warn someone about a possible danger or problem

ex.en: The doctor cautioned him against smoking.

cautious.en: careful to avoid danger or mistakes

ex.en: Be cautious when driving in the rain.

cautious.en: people who are careful and fearful of taking risks, as a group

ex.en: The cautious avoided investing during the crisis.
ex.ko: 신중한 사람들은 그 위기 동안 투자를 피했다.

center.en: the middle point or part of something

ex.en: They stood in the center of the room.

center.en: a building or place used for a particular activity

ex.en: She works out at the fitness center every day.

center.en: have something as the main focus or subject

ex.en: The discussion centered on climate change.
ex.ko: 그 논의는 기후 변화에 중점을 두었다.

ex.en: The new party describes itself as center in its politics.
ex.ko: 그 신생 정당은 스스로를 정치적으로 중도라고 소개한다.

chance.cue: 무언가를 할 수 있는 좋은 때

ex.en: This is your chance to prove yourself.

chance.en: the possibility that something will happen

ex.en: There's a good chance of rain tomorrow.

chance.en: take a risk in the hope of a good result

ex.en: I decided to chance it and invest my savings.
ex.ko: 나는 위험을 무릅쓰고 저축한 돈을 투자하기로 했다.

ex.en: They had a chance meeting at the airport.

chin.en: the part of the face below the mouth

ex.en: He rested his chin on his hands.

chin.en: do a chin-up exercise by pulling your body up until your chin reaches the bar

ex.en: He can chin the bar fifteen times in a row.
ex.ko: 그는 턱걸이를 한 번에 열다섯 번 할 수 있다.

choice.en: the act of choosing between two or more things

ex.en: It was a difficult choice to make.

choice.cue: 고를 수 있는 것들 중 하나
choice.en: one of a number of things you can choose from

ex.en: We don't have many choices for dinner tonight.
ex.ko: 오늘 밤 저녁 메뉴 선택지가 많지 않다.

choice.en: of very good quality; specially selected

ex.en: The restaurant only uses choice cuts of beef.
ex.ko: 그 식당은 최상급 소고기 부위만 사용한다.

clean.en: free from dirt or unwanted marks

ex.en: Keep your room clean.

clean.en: remove dirt or unwanted substances from something

ex.en: I need to clean my room.

clean.cue: 역도에서 바벨을 어깨까지 든 후 머리 위로 드는 동작
clean.en: (in weightlifting) the lift in which the barbell is raised to shoulder height before being pushed overhead

ex.en: He set a new record in the clean and jerk.
ex.ko: 그는 클린 앤드 저크에서 새 기록을 세웠다.

ex.en: I clean forgot about our appointment.

clear.en: remove things from a place to make it tidy or empty

ex.en: Please clear the table after dinner.
ex.ko: 저녁 식사 후에 식탁을 좀 치워 주세요.

ex.en: The instructions were clear and simple.

clear.en: (of weather or sky) free of clouds

ex.en: It was a clear day with a blue sky.
We had a clear view of the mountains.
ex.ko: 우리는 산이 탁 트이게 보이는 전망을 가졌다.

clear.en: in a way that is easy to hear or understand

ex.en: I can hear you loud and clear.

clock.en: a device that shows the time of day, usually not worn on the body

ex.en: The clock on the wall says three o'clock.

clock.en: measure how fast something moves or how long something takes

ex.en: The radar clocked his car at 120 kilometers per hour.
ex.ko: 레이더는 그의 차 속도를 시속 120킬로미터로 측정했다.

close.en: shut something so there is no opening

ex.en: Please close the door when you leave.
ex.ko: 나갈 때 문 좀 닫아 주세요.

ex.en: The hotel is close to the beach.

close.en: having a strong friendship or bond with someone

ex.en: She is very close to her sister.

close.en: the end of something such as an event or period of time

ex.en: The ceremony marked the close of the festival.
Stay close to me in the crowd.
ex.ko: 사람들 많은 곳에서는 내 가까이에 있어.

cloud.en: a visible mass of water droplets floating in the sky

ex.en: Dark clouds filled the sky before the storm.
ex.ko: 폭풍이 오기 전에 하늘이 먹구름으로 가득 찼다.

cloud.en: make something less clear, or make it hard to think clearly

ex.en: Anger clouded his judgment.

coat.cue: 추울 때 겉에 입는 긴 옷
coat.en: an outer garment with sleeves worn over other clothes for warmth

ex.en: Put on your coat before you go outside.

coat.en: a thin layer that covers a surface

ex.en: The wall needs another coat of paint.
ex.ko: 그 벽은 페인트를 한 번 더 칠해야 한다.

coat.en: cover the surface of something with a thin layer

ex.en: Coat the chicken with flour before frying.

code.en: a system of words, letters, or numbers used to send secret messages

ex.en: The spy sent a message in code.

code.en: a set of rules about how to behave

ex.en: Students must follow the school's dress code.
ex.ko: 학생들은 학교의 복장 규정을 따라야 한다.

code.en: the set of instructions that makes up a computer program

ex.en: She spent hours fixing the code.
ex.ko: 그녀는 코드를 고치는 데 몇 시간을 보냈다.

code.en: change information into a secret code

ex.en: The message was coded so enemies couldn't read it.
ex.ko: 그 메시지는 적들이 읽을 수 없도록 암호화되었다.

collar.en: the part of a shirt or coat that fits around the neck

ex.en: He wore a shirt with a stiff collar.
ex.ko: 그는 빳빳한 옷깃이 있는 셔츠를 입었다.

collar.en: a band placed around an animal's neck

ex.en: The dog's collar had a small bell on it.
ex.ko: 그 개의 목걸이에는 작은 방울이 달려 있었다.

ex.en: Police collared the suspect near the station.
ex.ko: 경찰은 역 근처에서 그 용의자를 붙잡았다.

colonial.en: relating to a colony or the period when a country controlled another country as a colony

ex.en: The museum has exhibits from the colonial era.
ex.ko: 그 박물관에는 식민지 시대의 전시물이 있다.

colonial.en: a person who lives in a colony

ex.en: The colonials built new towns along the coast.
ex.ko: 식민지 주민들은 해안을 따라 새 마을을 세웠다.

color.en: the quality that makes things look red, blue, green, etc.

ex.en: What color is your car?

color.en: add color to a picture or object

ex.en: The children colored the picture with crayons.

color.en: having colors, not just black and white

ex.en: They watched an old color film from the 1960s.

combine.en: join or mix two or more things together

ex.en: The recipe combines chocolate and orange flavors.
ex.ko: 그 레시피는 초콜릿과 오렌지 맛을 결합한다.

combine.en: a large farm machine that harvests and cleans grain

ex.en: The farmer drove the combine across the wheat field.

common.cue: 자주 발생하거나 널리 볼 수 있는
common.en: happening or existing often; found in many places

ex.en: This is a common mistake among beginners.

common.en: shared by two or more people or things

ex.en: They share a common interest in music.
ex.ko: 그들은 음악에 대한 공통의 관심사를 가지고 있다.

common.en: an area of open public land in a town, especially in Britain

ex.en: Children were playing football on the village common.
ex.ko: 아이들이 마을 공유지에서 축구를 하고 있었다.

concentrate.en: direct all your attention and effort toward one thing

ex.en: Try to concentrate on your homework.

concentrate.en: make a liquid stronger or thicker by removing water

ex.en: The factory concentrates the juice before shipping it.
ex.ko: 그 공장은 배송 전에 주스를 농축시킨다.

concentrate.en: a substance that has been made stronger by removing water

ex.en: This juice is made from orange concentrate.

conservative.en: opposed to change and holding traditional values

ex.en: Her parents have quite conservative views.
ex.ko: 그녀의 부모님은 꽤 보수적인 견해를 가지고 있다.

conservative.en: cautious, tending to underestimate rather than overestimate

ex.en: A conservative estimate puts the cost at one million dollars.
ex.ko: 신중하게 추정해도 비용은 백만 달러에 이른다.

conservative.cue: 변화를 꺼리고 전통적 가치를 지지하는 사람
conservative.en: a person who prefers traditional values and is cautious about change

ex.en: He has always been a conservative when it comes to money.
ex.ko: 그는 돈에 관해서는 항상 보수적인 사람이었다.

content.en: satisfied with what you have; not wanting more

ex.en: She seems content with her simple life.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자신의 소박한 삶에 만족하는 것 같다.

content.en: the information or ideas contained in something such as a book, speech, or website

ex.en: The content of the report was very detailed.

content.en: the amount of a substance contained in something

ex.en: This cereal has a high sugar content.

content.cue: 부족하지만 만족하며 받아들이다(격식체, content oneself with)
content.en: make yourself satisfied with something, even if it's not everything you want (formal)

ex.en: She had to content herself with a small apartment.

convert.en: change something from one form, system, or purpose to another

ex.en: They converted the old factory into apartments.

convert.en: change your religion, or persuade someone else to change theirs

ex.en: He converted to Islam after moving abroad.
ex.ko: 그는 해외로 이주한 후 이슬람교로 개종했다.

convert.en: a person who has changed their religion or beliefs

ex.en: She is a recent convert to vegetarianism.

core.en: the central or innermost part of something

ex.en: The Earth's core is extremely hot.

core.en: the most important or essential part of an idea, problem, or plan

ex.en: Trust is at the core of any good relationship.

core.en: remove the central part of a fruit, such as the seeds

ex.en: Core the apples before baking them.

corner.en: the place where two streets meet

ex.en: Turn left at the corner.

corner.en: the place where two walls or edges meet inside a room or object

ex.en: The lamp is in the corner of the room.

corner.en: force a person or animal into a place they cannot escape from

ex.en: The cat cornered the mouse behind the fridge.
ex.ko: 고양이는 그 쥐를 냉장고 뒤로 몰아넣었다.

costume.en: special clothes worn to look like a different character or historical period

ex.en: She wore a witch costume for Halloween.

costume.en: the traditional style of clothes worn by people from a particular country or period

ex.en: Dancers performed in traditional Korean costume.
The director costumed the actors as medieval knights.
ex.ko: 감독은 배우들에게 중세 기사 의상을 입혔다.

couch.en: a comfortable seat for more than one person

ex.en: He fell asleep on the couch.

couch.en: express something in a particular style of language (formal)

ex.en: The refusal was couched in polite language.

counter.en: a long flat surface where you pay for goods or are served

ex.en: She placed her items on the counter.

counter.en: respond to an argument, criticism, or attack

ex.en: She countered his argument with strong evidence.
ex.ko: 그녀는 확실한 증거로 그의 주장에 반박했다.

counter.en: in the opposite direction to, or in contrast with

ex.en: His actions ran counter to what he had promised.
ex.ko: 그의 행동은 그가 약속했던 것과 반대로 흘러갔다.

counter.en: opposing or working against something else

ex.en: They launched a counter offensive against the invaders.
ex.ko: 그들은 침략자들에 맞서 반격 작전을 개시했다.

couple.en: two people who are married or in a romantic relationship

ex.en: The couple got married last summer.

couple.en: a small number of things or people, usually two or a few

ex.en: I'll be back in a couple of minutes.

couple.cue: 두 가지가 함께 결합되다(격식체, coupled with)
couple.en: join or combine with something else (formal, often 'coupled with')

ex.en: Low pay, coupled with long hours, led many to quit.
ex.ko: 낮은 임금에 긴 근무 시간까지 겹쳐 많은 사람이 퇴사했다.

course.en: a series of lessons on a particular subject

ex.en: She is taking a course in French.

course.en: the way something develops or progresses over time

ex.en: Things changed during the course of the year.

course.en: one part of a meal served separately

ex.en: We had a three-course dinner.
ex.ko: 우리는 세 가지 코스로 된 저녁을 먹었다.

course.en: used to say something is obviously true or expected (in the phrase 'of course')

ex.en: Of course, I'll help you with your homework.

court.en: a place where legal trials happen and justice is decided

ex.en: The case will go to court next month.
ex.ko: 그 사건은 다음 달에 법정에 간다.

court.en: a marked area used for playing sports like tennis or basketball

ex.en: They played tennis on the court.

court.en: try to gain someone's favor or support

ex.en: The company is courting new investors.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 새로운 투자자들의 호감을 사려 애쓰고 있다.

credit.cue: 나중에 갚기로 하고 돈을 빌리는 것
credit.en: an arrangement to pay for something at a later time

ex.en: I bought the TV on credit.
ex.ko: 나는 그 TV를 신용 거래로 샀다.

credit.en: money that a bank or company allows you to borrow

ex.en: The bank increased her credit limit.

credit.en: public recognition for doing something good

ex.en: She deserves credit for her hard work.
ex.ko: 그녀는 노력에 대한 공로를 인정받을 자격이 있다.

credit.en: say that someone is responsible for an achievement

ex.en: She is credited with inventing the device.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 장치를 발명한 공로를 인정받는다.

crop.en: a plant grown in large amounts, especially for food

ex.en: Farmers here grow crops like wheat and corn.
ex.ko: 이곳 농부들은 밀과 옥수수 같은 작물을 재배한다.

crop.en: a group of people or things that appear or are produced at the same time

ex.en: This year's crop of graduates is especially talented.

crop.en: cut off part of something, such as a photo or hair

ex.en: She cropped the photo to remove the background.

crowd.en: a large number of people gathered together

ex.en: A crowd gathered outside the stadium.

crowd.en: a group of friends who spend time together (informal)

ex.en: She hangs out with a different crowd now.
ex.ko: 그녀는 요즘 다른 친구 무리와 어울린다.

crowd.en: gather together in a small space in large numbers

ex.en: Fans crowded around the singer after the show.
ex.ko: 공연이 끝난 후 팬들이 그 가수 주위로 몰려들었다.

crown.en: an ornamental headpiece worn by a king or queen

ex.en: The queen wore a golden crown.

crown.en: the title or position of being the champion or best

ex.en: She won the crown at the beauty pageant.

crown.en: an artificial covering placed over a damaged tooth

ex.en: The dentist put a crown on his broken tooth.
ex.ko: 치과의사는 그의 부러진 치아에 크라운을 씌웠다.

crown.en: officially make someone king or queen by placing a crown on their head

ex.en: He was crowned king at the age of eighteen.

cup.cue: 마실 때 쓰는 손잡이 있는 그릇
cup.en: a small container with a handle, used for drinking

ex.en: He drank a cup of tea.

cup.en: a unit used to measure an amount of food in cooking

ex.en: Add two cups of flour.

cup.en: form your hands into the shape of a cup

ex.en: He cupped his hands to drink from the stream.
ex.ko: 그는 시냇물을 마시려고 손을 오므려 컵 모양으로 만들었다.

cut.en: divide something into pieces with a sharp tool

ex.en: She cut the paper with scissors.

cut.en: a wound made by something sharp

ex.en: He has a cut on his finger.

cut.en: reduce the amount, size, or number of something

ex.en: The company cut prices by ten percent.

cut.en: (of flowers) cut from the plant to be displayed, not growing

ex.en: She arranged the cut flowers in a vase.

cut off.en: stop the supply or connection of something

ex.en: The storm cut off power to the whole town.

cut off.en: (of a phone call) be suddenly disconnected

ex.en: We got cut off in the middle of the call.

cut off.en: isolated from other people or places

ex.en: The village felt cut off from the rest of the world.
ex.ko: 그 마을은 세상과 단절된 듯한 느낌이었다.

dam.en: a large wall built to hold back water and control its flow

ex.en: The dam provides electricity for the whole region.
ex.ko: 그 댐은 그 지역 전체에 전기를 공급한다.

dam.en: block the flow of water with a dam

ex.en: Beavers dammed the stream with sticks and mud.

damn.cue: 화나거나 짜증날 때 명사 앞에 붙이는 (비속어)
damn.en: used before a noun to express anger or annoyance (informal, mildly rude)

ex.en: I can't find my damn keys.

damn.en: used to emphasize something (informal, mildly rude)

ex.en: It's damn cold outside today.

damn.cue: 누군가에게 안 좋은 일이 생기길 바라며
damn.en: wish that something bad will happen to someone (informal)

ex.en: She damned him for ruining her plans.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자신의 계획을 망친 그를 저주했다.

damn.cue: 조금도 신경 쓰지 않는다는 뜻으로(관용구 'not give a damn')
damn.en: something of very little value or importance (used in expressions like 'not give a damn')

ex.en: He doesn't give a damn what people think.
ex.ko: 그는 사람들이 어떻게 생각하든 조금도 신경 쓰지 않는다.

ex.en: The room was too dark to read in.
ex.ko: 그 방은 너무 어두워서 책을 읽을 수 없었다.

dark.en: (of a color) deep and strong rather than pale

ex.en: She wore a dark blue jacket.
She is afraid of the dark.
The plant in the corner is dead.
I'm dead after that long shift.
ex.ko: 그 긴 근무를 마치고 나니 몹시 지쳤다.

ex.en: You're dead right about that.
ex.ko: 그것에 대해 네 말이 완전히 맞아.

dead.en: people who have died, as a group

ex.en: The memorial honors the dead of both wars.
ex.ko: 그 추모비는 두 전쟁의 전사자들을 기리고 있다.

deaf.en: unable to hear, wholly or in part

ex.en: He has been deaf since birth.
ex.ko: 그는 태어날 때부터 청각 장애가 있다.

deaf.ko: 들으려 하지 않는, 귀 기울이지 않는
deaf.en: unwilling to listen to or consider something

ex.en: She was deaf to all our warnings.
ex.ko: 그녀는 우리의 모든 경고를 들으려 하지 않았다.

deaf.en: people who cannot hear, as a group

ex.en: The center offers classes for the deaf.
ex.ko: 그 센터는 청각 장애인들을 위한 수업을 제공한다.

decent.en: of an acceptable standard or quality

ex.en: He got a decent score on the exam.

decent.en: having good moral standards and treating people fairly

ex.en: She is a decent, honest person.

decent.en: in a proper or fair way (informal)

ex.en: At least he treated her decent.

despair.en: a feeling of complete loss of hope

ex.en: She was filled with despair after losing her job.
Don't despair — things will get better.

desperate.en: feeling that you must have or do something very much, often because a situation is very serious

ex.en: He was desperate to find a job.

desperate.en: a person who is in urgent, frightened need of help (rare)

ex.en: The shelter helps desperates who have nowhere else to turn.
ex.ko: 그 보호소는 달리 갈 곳 없는 절박한 사람들을 돕는다.

detail.en: a small piece of information about something

ex.en: Please give me all the details of the trip.
ex.ko: 여행에 대한 모든 세부사항을 알려 주세요.

detail.en: describe something fully, giving all the facts

ex.en: The report detailed the causes of the accident.
ex.ko: 그 보고서는 사고 원인을 자세히 설명했다.

diagram.en: a drawing that explains how something works

ex.en: The diagram shows how the engine works.
ex.ko: 그 도표는 엔진이 어떻게 작동하는지 보여준다.

diagram.en: show something, such as a sentence's structure, using a diagram

ex.en: The teacher diagrammed the sentence on the board.
ex.ko: 선생님은 칠판에 그 문장을 도표로 나타냈다.

ex.en: Her grandfather died last year.

die.en: (of a machine or engine) stop working suddenly

ex.en: The car engine died in the middle of the road.

die.en: a small cube marked with spots from one to six, used in games (singular of 'dice')

ex.en: He rolled the die and moved his piece forward.
ex.ko: 그는 주사위를 굴려 자기 말을 앞으로 옮겼다.

disabled.en: having a physical or mental condition that limits movement or activity

ex.en: The parking spot is reserved for disabled drivers.
ex.ko: 그 주차 공간은 장애가 있는 운전자를 위해 예약되어 있다.

disabled.en: people who have a physical or mental condition that limits activity, as a group

ex.en: The building has better access for the disabled now.
ex.ko: 그 건물은 이제 장애인들을 위한 접근성이 개선되었다.

discredit.en: cause people to stop believing or trusting someone or something

ex.en: The scandal discredited the entire organization.
ex.ko: 그 스캔들은 그 조직 전체의 신뢰를 떨어뜨렸다.

discredit.en: a loss of reputation or trust

ex.en: His actions brought discredit to the whole team.
ex.ko: 그의 행동은 팀 전체에 불명예를 안겼다.

ex.en: The coach threw the disk across the field.

disk.en: a flat device used to store computer information

ex.en: She saved the file onto a disk.

disk.en: break up soil using a disk harrow (farming term)

ex.en: The farmer disked the field before planting.
ex.ko: 농부는 씨를 뿌리기 전에 써레로 밭을 갈았다.

dissolve.en: become mixed into a liquid completely

ex.en: Sugar dissolves quickly in hot water.

dissolve.en: come to an end, or officially bring something to an end

ex.en: Parliament was dissolved before the election.

dissolve.en: (in film) a technique where one scene gradually changes into the next

ex.en: The director used a slow dissolve between the two scenes.
ex.ko: 감독은 두 장면 사이에 느린 디졸브 기법을 사용했다.

divine.en: relating to or coming from God or a god

ex.en: They believed the king had divine authority.
ex.ko: 그들은 왕이 신성한 권위를 가졌다고 믿었다.

divine.en: God (used especially in religious or philosophical contexts)

ex.en: She felt a deep connection to the Divine during meditation.
ex.ko: 그녀는 명상 중에 신과 깊이 연결되어 있음을 느꼈다.

doctor.en: a person who is trained to treat sick or injured people

ex.en: She went to see the doctor about her cough.

doctor.en: change something dishonestly to deceive people

ex.en: The photo had been doctored to remove a person.
ex.ko: 그 사진은 한 사람을 지우려고 조작되었다.

document.en: a piece of written, printed, or electronic material that provides information

ex.en: Please sign this document.

document.en: record something in detail, often with photos or writing

ex.en: The photographer documented the war for ten years.
ex.ko: 그 사진작가는 10년 동안 그 전쟁을 기록했다.

dog.en: a common animal often kept as a pet, known for barking

ex.en: The dog ran after the ball.

dog.en: follow or trouble someone persistently, especially problems

ex.en: Injuries have dogged his career for years.
ex.ko: 부상이 수년간 그의 커리어를 계속 괴롭혀 왔다.

ex.en: There is a small dot at the end of the sentence.
ex.ko: 그 문장 끝에는 작은 점이 있다.

dot.en: be scattered in small amounts across a place

ex.en: Small islands dot the coastline.
ex.ko: 작은 섬들이 해안선을 따라 점점이 흩어져 있다.

down.en: toward a lower place or position

ex.en: Please sit down.
He's been feeling a bit down lately.
ex.ko: 그는 요즘 기분이 좀 안 좋다.

down.en: the soft, fine feathers of a bird, used to fill jackets and pillows

ex.en: The jacket is filled with goose down.
ex.ko: 그 재킷은 거위 솜털로 채워져 있다.

ex.en: He downed his coffee before running out the door.
ex.ko: 그는 커피를 단숨에 들이켜고 문밖으로 뛰어나갔다.

downtown.en: in or to the central or main business area of a city

ex.en: We went downtown to do some shopping.

downtown.en: the central or main business area of a city

ex.en: Her office is in downtown Seoul.

downtown.en: located in or relating to the central business area of a city

ex.en: They rented a downtown apartment near the office.
ex.ko: 그들은 사무실 근처의 시내 아파트를 임대했다.

downward.en: moving or leading toward a lower place or level

ex.en: The company is on a downward trend.

downward.en: toward a lower place, position, or level

ex.en: The path leads downward toward the river.
ex.ko: 그 길은 강 쪽 아래로 이어진다.

dozen.en: a group of twelve of something

ex.en: She bought a dozen eggs.

dozen.en: used with a number to mean multiples of twelve

ex.en: She bought three dozen cookies for the party.

drag.en: pull something heavy along the ground with effort

ex.en: He dragged the suitcase across the floor.
ex.ko: 그는 여행 가방을 바닥에 끌고 갔다.

drag.en: move something on a computer screen by clicking and moving the mouse

ex.en: Drag the file into the folder.

drag.en: continue for longer than necessary, becoming boring

ex.en: The meeting dragged on for hours.
ex.ko: 그 회의는 몇 시간이나 질질 끌었다.

ex.en: Doing taxes is such a drag.
ex.ko: 세금 신고하는 건 정말 귀찮은 일이다.

drug.en: a substance used as medicine to treat illness

ex.en: This drug can reduce fever quickly.

drug.en: an illegal substance that affects the mind and can be addictive

ex.en: The police arrested him for selling drugs.
ex.ko: 경찰은 마약 판매 혐의로 그를 체포했다.

drug.en: secretly give someone a drug, often to harm them

ex.en: Someone drugged her drink at the party.
ex.ko: 누군가 파티에서 그녀의 음료에 약을 탔다.

drum.en: a musical instrument that you hit to make a sound

ex.en: He plays the drums in a band.

drum.en: tap something repeatedly to make a rhythmic sound

ex.en: She drummed her fingers on the desk while waiting.
ex.ko: 그녀는 기다리는 동안 책상을 손가락으로 두드렸다.

due.en: required to be paid or done by a certain time

ex.en: The rent is due on the first of the month.
The train is due to arrive at noon.

due.cue: 회비처럼 정기적으로 내야 하는 돈(주로 복수형 dues)
due.en: money that must be paid regularly, such as membership fees

ex.en: Members must pay their annual dues by January.
The lecture was so dull that I almost fell asleep.
ex.ko: 그 강의는 너무 지루해서 나는 거의 잠들 뻔했다.

dull.en: not sharp; not easy to cut with

ex.en: This knife is too dull to cut the bread.
ex.ko: 이 칼은 너무 무뎌서 빵을 자를 수 없다.

ex.en: The sky was a dull gray all day.

dull.en: make a feeling, such as pain, less intense

ex.en: The medicine dulled the pain in his leg.
ex.ko: 그 약은 그의 다리 통증을 무디게 했다.

earnest.en: serious and sincere about what you say or do

ex.en: He made an earnest attempt to apologize.

earnest.cue: 계약을 확정 짓기 위해 미리 내는 보증금(부동산 용어)
earnest.en: a sum of money paid in advance to show you are serious about a deal (especially in real estate)

ex.en: They paid an earnest deposit on the house.
ex.ko: 그들은 그 집에 대한 계약금을 지불했다.

ex.en: The birds migrated eastward for the winter.
ex.ko: 그 새들은 겨울을 나기 위해 동쪽으로 이동했다.

eastward.en: moving or facing toward the east

ex.en: They began their eastward journey at dawn.
ex.ko: 그들은 새벽에 동쪽으로 향하는 여정을 시작했다.

easy.en: not difficult to do or understand

ex.en: The test was easy for her.

easy.en: in a relaxed or gentle way (informal, same as 'easily')

ex.en: Take it easy — you don't need to rush.

edge.en: the outer or furthest part of something

ex.en: She stood at the edge of the cliff.

edge.en: a slight advantage over someone else

ex.en: Their experience gives them the edge in the competition.
ex.ko: 그들의 경험이 그 경쟁에서 우위를 준다.

edge.en: move slowly and carefully in a particular direction

ex.en: She edged closer to the door.
